plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-389 RP12.P09.M04.S01-RP12.P09.M05.S20 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-389 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP12.P09 workflow permission slice: the full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice cycle (twenty rows) and the M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first twenty rows) as two descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-390 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-389 is a descriptor-only billing-core pack (RP12.P09.M04.S01-RP12.P09.M05.S20, 40 units, risk B) covering the Secondary Workflow Slice (M04, 20 rows) and Permission And Audit Binding (M05, 20 rows). Review conducted read-only via Read/Grep/Glob across the live closeout-pack-plan, review baseline/request/packet, changed-file-scope, manifest, command-evidence, contract projection, source (registry.js/service.js/validators.js/index.js), tests (model.test.js), and the contract validator. The pack does not overclaim: manifest and command-evidence both report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review, correctly deferring runtime opening and any enterprise-trust claim to CP00-390 under human approval. Scope is consistent end-to-end: plan (line 412), baseline, manifest, and BILLING_CORE_CP389_PACK_BINDING agree on pack_id, risk B, 40 units, range, deviation_from_plan=false, deliverable distribution 6/4/2/2/26, and micro distribution 20/20. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code (freezeCp389Result sets ~30 guard flags false), mirrored in the contract no_write_attestation, and asserted by tests and the validator's deepEqual of the contract projection against the generated descriptor; the code path is pure frozen-object construction with no I/O, DB, network, or fixtures. The authority boundary is intact (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true), the Claude packet is read-only with Read/Grep/Glob and lists CP178+ invalid-evidence blockers, and the Hermes H12 packet is a deterministic evidence descriptor only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-evidence readiness is valid (auth preflight exit 0, 1228-byte valid JSON; no auth failure, 0-byte, or malformed output). Unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly isolated from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; pack is supported for closeout, with final enterprise-trust approval remaining a human responsibility.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-389 billing core p09 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
